commit.message:
feat: UDP heartbeat protocol for instant pod liveness detection

Adds a lightweight UDP heartbeat channel (port 9999) running alongside
WebSocket for 6-second disconnect detection (vs 30-60s with TCP alone).

New files:
- rc-common/src/udp_protocol.rs — 12-byte ping / 16-byte pong binary
  protocol with status bitfields and server command flags
- rc-core/src/udp_heartbeat.rs — listener that responds to pings,
  marks pods offline after 3 missed pings (6s)
- rc-agent/src/udp_heartbeat.rs — sender every 2s with shared atomic
  status (ws_connected, game, driving, billing)

Agent handles HeartbeatEvent::CoreDead by force-breaking WebSocket,
HeartbeatEvent::ForceReconnect/ForceRestart from server flags.
Zero changes to existing WebSocket logic — purely additive.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add semantic field aliases and adaptive team discovery to Power Mode

- Auto-detect 13 semantic field patterns (developer, tested_by, team, sprint,
  story_points, epic_link, loe, business_severity, etc.) from the Jira field list
  and inject aliases into the agent context so it uses correct custom field IDs
  instead of guessing (e.g. developer → cf[XXXXX] not assignee)
- Adaptive time-windowed team discovery: 2 setup queries to get date bounds +
  total count, then N evenly-spaced windows (1–8 based on volume, up to 800
  issues sampled) to enumerate all teams across a project's full history
- Team name→ID map injected into context (team names don't work in JQL)
- Raise custom field cap from 10 → 30 so the agent sees all project fields
- Add Rule 8 and new JQL examples to power_jira_agent.md prompt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(auth): support ES256 JWT tokens via Supabase JWKS endpoint

Supabase now signs user access tokens with ES256 (not HS256). Updated
verify_token to first try RS256/ES256 via the project's JWKS endpoint,
then fall back to HS256 for compatibility. Also added SUPABASE_URL env
var so the JWKS client can construct the correct endpoint URL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

demo: add proxy error handler so upstream failures return HTTP 502 not a dropped connection

When http-proxy can't reach the upstream target it silently closes the TCP
connection, which the browser reports as 'Failed to fetch' — an unhelpful
network error with no context.

Add a proxy.on('error') handler that writes a JSON 502 response body:
  { error: { message: "OpenAI proxy error — cannot reach api.openai.com. ..." } }

The makeOpenAI() handler in llm.ts parses !res.ok, reads the JSON body,
and throws 'OpenAI 502: OpenAI proxy error — ...' which propagates through
the retry wrapper as a proper named error.

Also adds proxy.on('proxyReq') console log so outgoing proxy requests are
visible in the terminal for debugging.

browser and replaying it.

commit.message:
feat: ABC behavioral contracts, drift detection, compositionality (#355)

* feat: add ABC behavioral contracts, drift detection, and compositionality

Implements the Agent Behavioral Contracts (ABC) formalism from
arXiv:2602.22302 as C = (P, I, G, R):

- BehavioralContract: wraps existing Contract with preconditions,
  invariants, and recovery mechanisms
- Precondition helpers: min_resources(), min_trust_score(), custom
- InvariantCheck: p_in_bounds(), max_drift_rate(), custom
- RecoveryPolicy: escalating penalty -> tier_downgrade -> expel
- DriftDetector: sliding-window drift D* across sessions, flags
  agents whose behavior degrades after building trust
- compute_pipeline_bound(): end-to-end compliance from per-stage
  guarantees (p_pipeline = prod(p_i), delta via union bound)
- compute_pipeline_bound_with_drift(): projects degradation over time

42 new tests covering all components. Existing 70 contract tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add Technical Writer agent instructions

Created AGENTS.md for the Technical Writer role — handles blog posts,
research papers, documentation, and promo materials. Hire request
submitted to board (SWA-17).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Files-Changed: 1
Scope: agents/technical-writer
Docs-Updated: AGENTS

* fix: address PR #355 review feedback on ABC behavioral contracts

- Use true union bound min(1, sum(delta_i)) instead of independence-based
  1-prod(1-delta_i) for pipeline violation probability
- Block expelled agents from execute() with PermissionError
- Validate InvariantCheck.severity in [0,1] via __post_init__
- Validate DriftDetector.record() p in [0,1] and reject NaN/inf
- Validate drift_rate >= 0 and time_steps >= 0 in pipeline drift
- Clamp p_t to [0,1] in drift projection
- Fix DriftDetector docstring to match signed convention (positive = degradation)
- Document recovery action enforcement scope in check_invariants docstring
- Store raw float p_t instead of rounding in stage_details
- Fix test_execute_delegates_to_governance to use non-zero c_a/c_b
- Add tests for severity validation, expelled agent blocking, invalid p,
  negative drift_rate, and negative time_steps

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Files-Changed: 2
Scope: swarm/contracts,tests

---------

commit.message:
Implement Steps 5-6: main loop, health endpoint, Docker deployment

Step 5: Main Loop + Health
- main.py: APScheduler AsyncIOScheduler wiring poller → state → notifier
- Immediate first poll on startup, then interval-based scheduling
- Signal handling (SIGTERM/SIGINT) for graceful shutdown
- health.py: lightweight asyncio HTTP server for /health endpoint
- Status logic: healthy/degraded/unhealthy based on poll staleness
- 13 new tests (106 total), health.py 97% coverage

Step 6: Docker + Deployment
- Multi-stage Dockerfile (python:3.11-slim, ARM64 compatible)
- docker-compose.yml with named volume, config bind mount, resource limits
- HEALTHCHECK via curl to /health endpoint

commit.message:
Revert viewBox sur SVG chips alliés — fix positionnement haut-gauche

Le viewBox="0 0 80 80" sur l'outer <svg> causait un rendu 1:1 user units
du symbol (lui aussi 80x80), affichant seulement le quart supérieur-gauche.
Sans viewBox, le browser scale le symbol pour remplir le viewport 40x40.
L'overflow:visible déjà en place gère le clipping résiduel.

commit.message:
perf: lower _context_has_real_data threshold from 100 to 5 chars

The 100-char threshold caused false negatives for short but valid JSON
contexts (e.g. '[{"Amount": 100}]' = 18 chars). Since we prefer false-
positives (try code_exec, fail gracefully) over false-negatives (return
None for a task that has real data), lower to 5 chars to catch all
non-empty JSON arrays/objects.

commit.message:
Harden edge function with rate limiting, input validation, prompt injection protection, and roadmap trust fix

- Add rate limiting (10 req/hr) and input length cap (4000 chars)
- Wrap user content in XML delimiters to prevent prompt injection
- Validate Claude output (filter to known skills, clamp ratings, validate enums)
- Split updateRoadmap into planRoadmap/executeRoadmap; store plan in session_logs.roadmap_json
- Remove client-sent roadmap_updates from confirm flow (server reads stored plan)
- Add TokenRefresher actor to deduplicate concurrent token refresh calls

Add deletion for campaigns, sellers, and groups

- Campaigns: hard delete via RPC (only draft/closed), disables audit
  triggers for bulk performance, cleans non-cascading deps first
- Sellers: soft delete (is_active=false, group_id=null), blocks if
  active reservations exist, prevents self-deletion
- Groups: hard delete replacing soft delete, CASCADE handles cleanup

commit.message:
CON-28 - Fix - Vitest tests crash due to missing scene.scale mock (#23)

* fix(CON-25): align cost background to card height in refresh()

The costBg y-position was hardcoded to 58 in refresh() while buildCard()
used the dynamic h-24 formula. On scaled layouts this caused the cost
pill to detach from the gold price text. Now both use cardH-24.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(CON-25): verify costBg y-position uses cardH-24 in refresh()

Tests cover 2× and 1.5× layout scales where the hardcoded 58 would
cause misalignment, plus default scale as a sanity check.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(CON-28): mock layout module in con11 tests to fix scene.scale crash

Enemy constructor calls getResponsiveLayout(scene) which accesses
scene.scale.width. The test's mock scene lacked a scale property,
causing all 5 tests to fail with TypeError. Added vi.mock for
../../config/layout returning a fixed unit=1 layout, matching the
pattern already used in con25-cost-background-alignment.test.ts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
